generatedSentence: The disgraced astronaut, a former inquisitor in a tattered tailcoat, flunked his last mission. Nursing a java hangover, his tall coffee tasted bitter as the bright sunrise painted the rustic sky. He watched a gosling near an empty, sacred tree, a moose carving nearby, pondering the enchanted locations he had to initiate. His pyjama-clad form yearned for the washing of a new start, his nunchaku and helmet ready for jujutsu training. An underhanded plot sought to undermine his generational quest, a rival faction attempting to ransack his secrets. The lackey, a gonzo reject from the Kremlin, honked a warning, a deflection as he tried to uncover juicy volts of data. It was an example of the wrath they wielded, electrifying the subject with a cleaver-like force. Inhaling the leaked intel, he knew neither validation nor victory would come easily, only gore awaited those who warble their defiance. In the shipway, a major jeweler, wealthy from ancient, underhanded revenue, stood accused. The draft from the open bay revealed a transparent layer of ultraviolet light, a novelty that would mimic a singularity. The villain, a winner with a newly shaved haircut, whomped his remaining unbent postal associate. He thought his secrets unobtainable, but a vector of light from the hero's mouth revealed the grave truth. He had an unfailing supply of nitrous, which proved handy as his modern weapon, a nickelled teapot, spun along the highway. The tricky victim, revealed by the ultraviolet glow, knew his game was up. This solid win was not just bingo for the hero, but a rich banquet, leaving the villain fracked and exposed, one of the extras consuming his pastas in the pickups, mounting a jaggy retreat.
panelSentences: The disgraced astronaut, a former inquisitor in a tattered tailcoat, flunked his last mission. Nursing a java hangover, his tall coffee tasted bitter as the bright sunrise painted the rustic sky. He watched a gosling near an empty, sacred tree, a moose carving nearby, pondering the enchanted locations he had to initiate. His pyjama-clad form yearned for the washing of a new start, his nunchaku and helmet ready for jujutsu training.

An underhanded plot sought to undermine his generational quest, a rival faction attempting to ransack his secrets. The lackey, a gonzo reject from the Kremlin, honked a warning, a deflection as he tried to uncover juicy volts of data. It was an example of the wrath they wielded, electrifying the subject with a cleaver-like force. Inhaling the leaked intel, he knew neither validation nor victory would come easily, only gore awaited those who warble their defiance.

The draft from the open bay revealed a transparent layer of ultraviolet light, a novelty that would mimic a singularity. The villain, a winner with a newly shaved haircut, whomped his remaining unbent postal associate. He thought his secrets unobtainable, but a vector of light from the hero's mouth revealed the grave truth.

He had an unfailing supply of nitrous, which proved handy as his modern weapon, a nickelled teapot, spun along the highway. The tricky victim, revealed by the ultraviolet glow, knew his game was up. This solid win was not just bingo for the hero, but a rich banquet, leaving the villain fracked and exposed, one of the extras consuming his pastas in the pickups, mounting a jaggy retreat.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a grizzled astronaut wearing a distressed tailcoat and a polished silver chrome helmet. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, in pajamas, sips coffee while looking out at a bright, rustic sunrise, a nunchaku hanging on a nearby wall, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A shadowy figure, a lackey in a Kremlin-style uniform, honking a warning from a futuristic vehicle, its headlights electrifying the scene, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The protagonist, now in full gear, uses a transparent ultraviolet beam from his helmet to reveal a wealthy jeweler with a shaved haircut, standing accused in a neon-lit shipway, close-up on the reveal. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The protagonist, riding a futuristic motorcycle on a jaggy highway, holding a nickelled teapot that glows with energy, chasing a fleeing pickup truck, dynamic action shot.

definitions.definition: Eager search.
To loot or pillage. See also sack.
To make a vigorous and thorough search of (a place, person) with a view to stealing something, especially when leaving behind a state of disarray.
definitions.example: to ransack a house for valuables

definitions.definition: To examine carefully; to investigate.
To violate; to ravish; to deflower.
An increase in wages or salary; a rise (UK).
definitions.example: The boss gave me a raise.

definitions.definition: A shoulder exercise in which the arms are elevated against resistance.
A shot in which the delivered stone bumps another stone forward.
A bet that increases the previous bet.
(physical) To cause to rise; to lift or elevate.
definitions.example: to raise your hand if you want to say something; to raise your walking stick to defend yourself

definitions.definition: To create, increase or develop.
definitions.example: We need to raise the motivation level in the company.

definitions.definition: To establish contact with (e.g., by telephone or radio).
definitions.example: Despite all the call congestion, she was eventually able to raise the police.

definitions.definition: To respond to a bet by increasing the amount required to continue in the hand.
definitions.example: John bet, and Julie raised, requiring John to put in more money.

definitions.definition: To exponentiate, to involute.
definitions.example: Two raised to the fifth power equals 32.

definitions.definition: (of a verb) To extract (a subject or other verb argument) out of an inner clause.
(of a vowel) To produce a vowel with the tongue positioned closer to the roof of the mouth.
To increase the nominal value of (a cheque, money order, etc.) by fraudulently changing the writing or printing in which the sum payable is specified.
To throw (an exception).
definitions.example: A division by zero will raise an exception.

definitions.definition: To dig underneath (something), to make a passage for destructive or military purposes; to sap.
To weaken or work against; to hinder, sabotage.
definitions.example: The war efforts were undermined by the constant bickering between the allies.

definitions.definition: To erode the base or foundation of something, e.g. by the action of water.
A weapon originating from Okinawa, Japan, consisting of two sticks joined by a chain or cord.
The skill of using this weapon in martial arts.
A particular point or place in physical space.
An act of locating.
An apartheid-era urban area populated by non-white people; township.
A leasing on rent.
A contract for the use of a thing, or service of a person, for hire.
The marking out of the boundaries, or identifying the place or site of, a piece of land, according to the description given in an entry, plan, map, etc.
To employ hydraulic fracturing (fracking)
Fuck.
Something additional, such as an item above and beyond the ordinary school curriculum, or added to the usual charge on a bill.
An extra edition of a newspaper, which is printed outside of the normal printing cycle.
definitions.example: Extra, extra! Read all about it!

definitions.definition: A run scored without the ball having hit the striker's bat - a wide, bye, leg bye or no ball.
A supernumerary or walk-on in a film or play.
Something of an extra quality or grade.
Having lots of juice.
definitions.example: a juicy peach

definitions.definition: (of a story, etc.) Exciting; titillating.
definitions.example: I do not keep up with all the latest juicy rumors.

definitions.definition: (of a blow, strike, etc.) Strong, painful.
Voluptuous, curvy, thick.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V
A circular tread; a gait by which a horse going sideways round a centre makes two concentric tracks.
A sudden movement to avoid a thrust.
To remove a cover from.
definitions.example: The model railway was uncovered.

definitions.definition: To reveal the identity of.
definitions.example: The murderer has finally been uncovered.

definitions.definition: To show openly; to disclose; to reveal.
To remove one's hat or cap as a mark of respect.
To expose the genitalia.
To expose (lines of formation of troops) successively by the wheeling to right or left of the lines in front.
A squarish, heavy knife used by butchers for hacking through bones, etc.
(Bahamas) A type of clave, or rhythm stick, a concussive musical instrument used in traditional Bahamian music.
(metaphoric) The act of eliminating someone or something, especially when done by someone with a history of other eliminations; a dismissal, rejection, or removal.
Of, pertaining to, or changing over generations.
The act of validating something.
Something, such as a certificate, that validates something; attestation, authentication, confirmation, proof or verification.
The process whereby others confirm the validity of one's emotions.
The process of identifying a new prisoner's gang affiliation.
The time of day when the sun appears above the eastern horizon.
definitions.example: I'll meet you at the docks at sunrise.

definitions.definition: The change in color of the sky at dawn.
definitions.example: Did you see the beautiful sunrise this morning?

definitions.definition: Any great awakening.
definitions.example: It was the sunrise of her spirit.

definitions.definition: Dough made from wheat and water and sometimes mixed with egg and formed into various shapes; often sold in dried form and typically boiled for eating.
A dish or serving of pasta.
A type of pasta.
To use a car horn.
definitions.example: They stood and observed how long it took for the other cars to honk.

definitions.definition: To make a loud, harsh sound like a car horn.
To make the vocal sound of a goose.
To vomit: regurgitate the contents of one's stomach.
To have a bad smell.
To squeeze playfully, usually a breast or nose.
Similarly not.
definitions.example: Just as you would not correct it, neither would I.

definitions.definition: Not either one of two.
definitions.example: I've tried on both shirts, but neither fits properly.

definitions.definition: Not either (used with nor).
definitions.example: Neither now, nor ever will he forsake his mother.

definitions.definition: A footman, a liveried male servant.
A fawning, servile follower.
To attend, wait upon, serve obsequiously.
To toady, play the flunky.
An artist's brush used in oil and acrylic painting with a long ferrule and a flat, somewhat tapering bristle head.
Splendour; brightness
A person with a naturalistic worldview with no supernatural or mystical elements.
(in the plural) The high-beam intensity of motor vehicle headlamps.
definitions.example: Your brights are on.

definitions.definition: Visually dazzling; luminous, lucent, clear, radiant; not dark.
definitions.example: Could you please dim the light? It's far too bright.

definitions.definition: Having a clear, quick intellect; intelligent.
definitions.example: He's very bright. He was able to solve the problem without my help.

definitions.definition: Vivid, colourful, brilliant.
definitions.example: The orange and blue walls of the sitting room were much brighter than the dull grey walls of the kitchen.

definitions.definition: Happy, in good spirits.
definitions.example: I woke up today feeling so bright that I decided to have a little dance.

definitions.definition: Sparkling with wit; lively; vivacious; cheerful.
Illustrious; glorious.
Clear; transparent.
Manifest to the mind, as light is to the eyes; clear; evident; plain.
To supply electricity to; to charge with electricity.
definitions.example: to electrify a cable

definitions.definition: To cause electricity to pass through; to affect by electricity; to give an electric shock to.
definitions.example: to electrify a limb, or the body

definitions.definition: To adapt (a home, farm, village, city, industry, railroad) for electric power.
To strongly excite, especially by something delightful or inspiring; to thrill.
definitions.example: Her performance in the play electrified the audience.

definitions.definition: To become electric.
Something that is representative of all such things in a group.
Something that serves to illustrate or explain a rule.
Something that serves as a pattern of behaviour to be imitated (a good example) or not to be imitated (a bad example).
A person punished as a warning to others.
A parallel or closely similar case, especially when serving as a precedent or model.
An instance (as a problem to be solved) serving to illustrate the rule or precept or to act as an exercise in the application of the rule.
To be illustrated or exemplified (by).
A member of the crew of a spaceship or other spacecraft that travels beyond Earth's atmosphere, or someone trained to serve that purpose.
A returnee who frequently flies back and forth between Hong Kong and his/her adopted home country.
A person who inquires, especially searchingly or ruthlessly.
An official of the ecclesiastical court of the Inquisition.
A formal evening jacket with an extended back panel; a dress coat.
Any coat with similar tails.
Of a student, to fail a class; to not pass.
definitions.example: He flunked math, again.

definitions.definition: Of a teacher, to deny a student a passing grade.
definitions.example: Unsatisfied with Fred's progress, the teacher flunked him.

definitions.definition: To shirk (a task or duty).
To back out through fear. (Commonly in the phrase 'flunk it', the 'it' referring to a specific task avoided; sometimes without specific reference describing a person's attitude to life in general.)
A long, slender, chiefly terrestrial amphibian of the order Caudata, superficially resembling a lizard.
A creature much like a lizard that is resistant to and lives in fire (in which it is often depicted in heraldry), hence the elemental being of fire.
A metal utensil with a flat head which is heated and put over a dish to brown the top.
A small broiler (North America) or grill (Britain) that heats the food from above, used in professional cookery primarily for browning.
definitions.example: The chef first put the steak under the salamander to sear the outside.

definitions.definition: The pouched gopher, Geomys tuza, of the southern United States.
A large poker.
Solidified material in a furnace hearth.
Portable stove used to heat or dry buildings under construction.
The atmosphere above a given point, especially as visible from the ground during the day.
definitions.example: That year, a meteor fell from the sky.

definitions.definition: The part of the sky which can be seen from a specific place or at a specific time; its condition, climate etc.
definitions.example: I lay back under a warm Texas sky.

definitions.definition: Heaven.
definitions.example: This mortal has incurred the wrath of the skies.

definitions.definition: (theoretical physics) The set of all lightlike lines (or directions) passing through a given point in space-time.
In an art gallery, the upper rows of pictures that cannot easily be seen.
A cloud.
To hit, kick or throw (a ball) extremely high.
To clear (a hurdle, high jump bar, etc.) by a large margin.
To hang (a picture on exhibition) near the top of a wall, where it cannot be well seen.
To drink something from a container without one's lips touching the container.
A vibrant light blue colour, like that of the sky on a fine day.

A game of chance for two or more players, who mark off numbers on a grid as they are announced by the caller; the game is won by the first person to call out "bingo!" or "house!" after crossing off all numbers on the grid or in one line of the grid.
A win in such a game.
definitions.example: There were two bingos in the last game, so the players split the prize money.

definitions.definition: (Scrabble) A play where all seven of a player's letter tiles are played.
To play the game of bingo.
To give the winning cry of "bingo!" in a game.
To play all of one's seven tiles in one move in the game of Scrabble, earning a score bonus.
To return to base.
Just sufficient to return to base (or, alternatively, to divert to an alternative airfield). (also written Bingo or BINGO)
Used by players of bingo to claim a win.
Used when finding what one has been looking for or trying to recall, or on successful completion of a task.
Used to declare "You've just made my point!" or "My point exactly!"
(usually plural) A container, especially a bottle, whose contents have been used up, leaving it empty.
definitions.example: Put the empties out to be recycled.

definitions.definition: To make empty; to void; to remove the contents of.
definitions.example: The cinema emptied quickly after the end of the film.

definitions.definition: Of a river, duct, etc: to drain or flow toward an ultimate destination.
definitions.example: Salmon River empties on the W shore about 2 miles below Bear River.

definitions.definition: Devoid of content; containing nothing or nobody; vacant.
definitions.example: an empty purse; an empty jug; an empty stomach

definitions.definition: Containing no elements (as of a string, array, or set), opposed to being null (having no valid value).
Free; clear; devoid; often with of.
Having nothing to carry, emptyhanded; unburdened.
Destitute of effect, sincerity, or sense; said of language.

definitions.definition: Unable to satisfy; hollow; vain.

definitions.definition: Destitute of reality, or real existence; unsubstantial.

definitions.definition: Destitute of, or lacking, sense, knowledge, or courtesy.
definitions.example: empty brains; an empty coxcomb

definitions.definition: (of some female animals, especially cows and sheep) Not pregnant; not producing offspring when expected to do so during the breeding season.
definitions.example: Empty cow rates have increased in recent years.

definitions.definition: Producing nothing; unfruitful; said of a plant or tree.
definitions.example: an empty vine

definitions.definition: Inexhaustible
Changeless
Infallible
An electronic device for detecting sound, vibration, etc., such as one fitted to an electric guitar or record player.
A pickup truck.
(usually attributive) Impromptu or ad hoc, especially of sports games and teams made up of randomly selected players.
definitions.example: At lunch we had a game of pickup hockey.

definitions.definition: An instance of approaching someone and engaging in romantic flirtation and courting with the intent to pursue romance, a date, or a sexual encounter. See also pick-up line, pick-up joint, pickup artist.
definitions.example: Hey, thanks for the drink, but if this is a pickup, I'm not interested.

definitions.definition: A person successfully approached in this manner for romance or sex.
In various games, the fielding or hitting of a ball just after it strikes the ground.
An item that can be picked up by the player, conferring some benefit or effect; a power-up.
The act of a challenging party or candidate winning an electoral district held by an incumbent party or candidate. See also gain
definitions.example: The returns from the election show Apple Party candidate Jane Doe has made a pickup in the district of City West defeating Orange Party Incumbent Joe Smith

definitions.definition: The act of answering a telephone.
A relatively minor shot filmed or recorded after the fact to augment previous footage.
The act of collecting and taking away something or someone, usually in a vehicle.
To clean with water.
definitions.example: Dishwashers wash dishes much more efficiently than most humans.

definitions.definition: To move or erode by the force of water in motion.
definitions.example: Heavy rains wash a road or an embankment.

definitions.definition: To separate valuable material (such as gold) from worthless material by the action of flowing water.
To clean oneself with water.
definitions.example: I wash every morning after getting up.

definitions.definition: To cover with water or any liquid; to wet; to fall on and moisten.
definitions.example: Waves wash the shore.

definitions.definition: To move with a lapping or swashing sound; to lap or splash.
definitions.example: to hear the water washing

definitions.definition: To be eroded or carried away by the action of water.
To be cogent, convincing; to withstand critique.
To bear without injury the operation of being washed.
definitions.example: Some calicoes do not wash.

definitions.definition: To be wasted or worn away by the action of water, as by a running or overflowing stream, or by the dashing of the sea; said of road, a beach, etc.
To cover with a thin or watery coat of colour; to tint lightly and thinly.
To overlay with a thin coat of metal.
definitions.example: steel washed with silver

definitions.definition: To cause dephosphorization of (molten pig iron) by adding substances containing iron oxide, and sometimes manganese oxide.
To pass (a gas or gaseous mixture) through or over a liquid for the purpose of purifying it, especially by removing soluble constituents.
The action of the verb to wash
Clothing, bedlinen or soft furnishings that have been, are currently being, or are to be washed; laundry.
definitions.example: My mother used to do the washing on a Monday

definitions.definition: (often in the plural) The residue after an ore, etc, has been washed
definitions.example: The washings have a higher concentration of metal

definitions.definition: The liquid used to wash an ore.
A place where a precious metal found in gravel is separated from lighter material by washing.
definitions.example: the gold-washings, or silver-washings

definitions.definition: A thin covering or coat.
definitions.example: a washing of silver

definitions.definition: A fraudulent transaction in which the same stock is simultaneously bought and sold for the purpose of manipulating the market.
The covering of a piece with an infusible powder, which prevents it from sticking to its supports, while receiving the glaze.
Negative effects, such as headache or nausea, caused by previous drunkenness due to (excessive) consumption of alcohol.
definitions.example: I really enjoyed yesterday’s party, but now I have the biggest hangover – I’ll not be doing that again any time soon.

definitions.definition: Similar negative effects caused by previous excessive consumption of something else, such as a drug, coffee, sugar, etc.
An unpleasant relic left from prior events.
A sleeping arrangement, usually in homeless shelters, over a rope.
The largest member of the deer family (Alces americanus, sometimes included in Alces alces), of which the male has very large, palmate antlers.
definitions.example: We saw a moose at the edge of the woods.

definitions.definition: An ugly person.
A young goose.
A callow), or foolish and naive, young person.
A catkin on nut trees and pines.
Gonzo journalism or a journalist who produces such journalism.
A wild or crazy person.
Using an unconventional, exaggerated and highly subjective style, often when the reporter takes part in the events of the story.
Unconventional, bizarre, crazy.
(Russian architecture) A fortified, central complex found in various Russian cities.
To attract and delight, to charm.
To cast a spell upon (often one that attracts or charms).
To magically enhance or degrade an item.
Charmed, delighted, enraptured.
Under the influence of enchantment.
A greeting used when introduced to someone for the first time, especially by a man when introduced to a woman.
To enrich.
To become rich.
Wealthy: having a lot of money and possessions.
Having an intense fatty or sugary flavour.

definitions.definition: Plentiful, abounding, abundant, fulfilling.
definitions.example: a rich treasury; a rich entertainment; a rich crop

definitions.definition: Yielding large returns; productive or fertile; fruitful.
definitions.example: rich soil or land; a rich mine

definitions.definition: Composed of valuable or costly materials or ingredients; procured at great outlay; highly valued; precious; sumptuous; costly.
definitions.example: a rich endowment; a rich dress; rich silk or fur; rich presents

definitions.definition: Not faint or delicate; vivid.
definitions.example: a rich red colour

definitions.definition: Very amusing.
definitions.example: The scene was a rich one.

definitions.definition: Ridiculous, absurd, outrageous, preposterous.
Elaborate, having complex formatting, multimedia, or depth of interaction.
Of a fuel-air mixture, having less air than is necessary to burn all of the fuel; less air- or oxygen- rich than necessary for a stoichiometric reaction.
Trading at a price level which is high relative to historical trends, a similar asset, or (for derivatives) a theoretical value.
Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: To toss or lob with an underhand movement.
To trick, deceive or gull.
To excavate downward in successive steps or horizontal slices while positioned above on unbroken ore.
Devious people, collectively.
Done by moving the hand (and arm) from below.
Sly, dishonest, corrupt, cheating.
definitions.example: His underhanded trick backfired and he was disqualified.

definitions.definition: Insincere; sarcastic.
definitions.example: An underhanded compliment is actually criticism.

definitions.definition: Secret; surreptitious.
Understaffed.
In an underhanded manner.
An only child.
Alone in a category.
definitions.example: He is the only doctor for miles.

definitions.definition: Singularly superior; the best.
Without sibling; without a sibling of the same gender.
definitions.example: He is their only son, in fact, an only child.

definitions.definition: Mere.
Without others or anything further; exclusively.
definitions.example: My heart is hers, and hers only.

definitions.definition: No more than; just.
definitions.example: If there were only one more ticket!

definitions.definition: As recently as.
definitions.example: He left only moments ago.

definitions.definition: Used to express surprise or consternation at an action.
definitions.example: She's only gone and run off with the milkman!

definitions.definition: Introduces a disappointing or surprising outcome that renders futile something previously mentioned. See also only to, only for.
definitions.example: I helped him out only for him to betray me.

definitions.definition: Above all others; particularly.
Under the condition that; but.
definitions.example: You're welcome to borrow my bicycle, only please take care of it.

definitions.definition: But; except.
definitions.example: I would enjoy running, only I have this broken leg.

definitions.definition: To draw air into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.

To draw air or any form of gas (either in a pure form, or mixed with small particles in form of aerosols/smoke -sometimes stemming from a medicament) into the lungs, through the nose or mouth by action of the diaphragm.
To eat very quickly.
Inhalation
The act of deflecting or something deflected.
The deviation of a needle or other indicator from its previous position.
The sound of one who warbles; singing with trills or modulations.
In naval mine warfare, the process of varying the frequency of sound produced by a narrowband noisemaker to ensure that the frequency to which the mine will respond is covered.
To modulate a tone's frequency.
To sing like a bird, especially with trills.
To cause to quaver or vibrate.
To be quavered or modulated; to be uttered melodiously.
A beverage made by infusing the beans of the coffee plant in hot water.
A serving of this beverage.
The seeds of the plant used to make coffee, misnamed ‘beans’ due to their shape.
The powder made by roasting and grinding the seeds.
A tropical plant of the genus Coffea.
A pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
The end of a meal, when coffee is served.
definitions.example: He did not stay for coffee.

definitions.definition: To drink coffee.
Of a pale brown colour, like that of milk coffee.
Of a table: a small, low table suitable for people in lounge seating to put coffee cups on.
A blend of coffee imported from the island of Java.
Coffee in general.
A dance popular in France in the early 20th century.
(possibly nonstandard) Someone or something that is tall.
(of a person) Having a vertical extent greater than the average. For example, somebody with a height of over 6 feet would generally be considered to be tall.
definitions.example: Being tall is an advantage in basketball.

definitions.definition: (of a building, etc.) Having its top a long way up; having a great vertical (and often greater than horizontal) extent; high.
(of a story) Hard to believe, such as a tall story or a tall tale.
(of a cup of coffee) A cup of coffee smaller than grande, usually 8 ounces.
Obsequious; obedient.
Seemly; suitable; fitting, becoming, comely; attractive, handsome.
Bold; brave; courageous; valiant.
Fine; proper; admirable; great; excellent.
To allow fluid or gas to pass through an opening that should be sealed.
definitions.example: The faucet has been leaking since last month.

definitions.definition: (of a fluid or gas) To pass through an opening that should be sealed.
definitions.example: No one realized that propane gas was leaking from a rusty tank in the concession area, slowly filling the unventilated room.

definitions.definition: To disclose secret information surreptitiously or anonymously.
definitions.example: Someone must have leaked it to our competitors that the new product will be out soon.

definitions.definition: Conditional upon.

definitions.definition: Placed or situated under; lying below, or in a lower situation.

Characterized by solemn religious ceremony or religious use, especially, in a positive sense; consecrated, made holy.

definitions.definition: Religious; relating to religion, or to the services of religion; not secular
Spiritual; concerned with metaphysics.

Not to be profaned or violated; inviolable.

Solemnly devoted, in a bad sense, as to evil, vengeance, curse, or the like; accursed; baleful.

Country-styled or pastoral; rural.

definitions.definition: Simple; artless; unaffected.

An imitation.

Pertaining to mimicry; imitative.
Mock, pretended.

Precision maneuvering.

To move like a jellyfish.

To begin; to start.

Unpractised; untried; new.
Begun; commenced; introduced to, or instructed in, the rudiments; newly admitted.
Fast growing function based on Kruskal's tree theorem.

A perennial woody plant, not exactly defined, but differentiated from a shrub by its larger size (typically over a few meters in height) or growth habit, usually having a single (or few) main axis or trunk unbranched for some distance above the ground and a head of branches and foliage.

definitions.definition: Any plant that is reminiscent of the above but not classified as a tree (in any botanical sense).

definitions.definition: The structural frame of a saddle.

A recursive data structure in which each node has zero or more nodes as children.

Former; sometime.

The pulling force (tension) on couplers and draft gear during a slack stretched condition.

definitions.definition: A quantity that is requisitioned or drawn out from a larger population.

definitions.definition: A person whose job is making, repairing or selling jewelry.

definitions.definition: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
A substance in the fundamental state of matter that retains its size and shape without need of a container (as opposed to a liquid or gas).
A three-dimensional figure (as opposed to a surface, an area, or a curve).

definitions.definition: An article of clothing which is of a single color throughout.

definitions.definition: Large in size, quantity, or value.
Lacking holes, hollows or admixtures of other materials.

definitions.definition: Strong or unyielding.

definitions.definition: Excellent, of high quality, or reliable.

definitions.definition: Hearty; filling.

definitions.definition: Worthy of credit, trust, or esteem; substantial; not frivolous or fallacious.

Sound; not weak.

definitions.definition: Written as one word, without spaces or hyphens.

definitions.definition: Not having the lines separated by leads; not open.

definitions.definition: (of drawn lines) Continuous; unbroken; not dotted or dashed.

definitions.definition: Solidly.
Without spaces or hyphens.
